Local Government Architecture Model

LGAM

  • Overview
  • Capabilities
  • Business Areas
LGAM Viewpoints

Example architecture: Adult Social Care

About this view

Two approaches to visualising how a council's adult social care technology connects across LGAM layers — without implying a linear chain.

Each shows the same information: specific systems and services involved in delivering adult social care, arranged by LGAM layer. Any element can connect to any other element in any layer.

Option A: Zone layout

Layers shown as spatial zones — the citizen journey flows left-to-right across the top, enabling technology sits beneath.

Channels

Council website Contact centre Social worker visit

Interfaces

Citizen portal Professional portal Mobile app

Capabilities

Liquidlogic GOV.UK Forms GOV.UK Pay One Login Bookings

Business processes

Assessment Care planning Safeguarding Financial assessment Provider management

Integration

NHS Spine DWP API CQC API Azure Integration

Foundational Technology

Azure UK South Entra ID ServiceNow Azure Monitor

Security

Microsoft Defender Managed SOC OneTrust (DPIA)

Data

Citizen record LLPG Power BI Data Lake

Option B: Hub and spoke

The business area at the centre, with all connected technology radiating outward — showing that relationships are not hierarchical.

Adult Social Care Assessment, care planning, safeguarding, provider management

Channels

  • Council website
  • Contact centre
  • Social worker visit

Interfaces

  • Citizen portal
  • Professional portal
  • Mobile app

Capabilities

  • Liquidlogic (case management)
  • GOV.UK Forms (referrals)
  • GOV.UK Pay (contributions)
  • One Login (identity)
  • Bookings (respite/day care)

Corporate

  • Finance (care funding)
  • HR (workforce)
  • Legal (MCA, CoP)

Integration

  • NHS Spine
  • DWP benefits API
  • CQC Provider API
  • Azure Integration Services

Foundational Tech

  • Azure UK South
  • Entra ID
  • ServiceNow
  • Azure Monitor

Security

  • Microsoft Defender
  • Managed SOC
  • OneTrust (DPIA)

Data

  • Citizen master record
  • LLPG / Addresses
  • Power BI (ASC-CLD)
  • Azure Data Lake

Notes

  • Both views show the same information — specific systems involved in delivering adult social care, arranged by LGAM layer.
  • Neither implies a linear chain. Any element can connect to any element in any layer.
  • These are illustrative — your council would substitute its own systems.
  • For interactive connection mapping, see the LGAM Connection Editor.
